text: Trocars are placed in the abdominal wall for laparoscopic access. The surgeon performs a diagnostic laparoscopy, revealing two angulations in the gastric tube with adhesions. Using a monopolar hook, the surgeon begins dissection at the hiatus, lysing adhesions to free the gastric tube.

The surgeon mobilizes the stomach and dissects a posterior window around the gastric tube. Using two stapler loads, the stomach is transected. The transverse colon is retracted to identify the angle of Treitz. The jejunum is measured fifty centimeters from the angle of Treitz and transected with a stapler. The alimentary limb is measured to be one hundred and twenty centimeters, and enterotomies are performed using a monopolar hook.

The surgeon performs a side-to-side anastomosis using a stapler, followed by oversewing the enterotomies in two layers with 3-0 absorbable sutures. The gastrojejunostomy begins with a continuous posterior outer layer of sutures, followed by a continuous inner-posterior layer. After completing the posterior layer, an inner-anterior layer is started, with sutures joining from opposite sides in the middle. The outer anterior layer is completed to finalize the anastomosis.
